name: CTX Ontology (ContextTypes — Organisational Context Taxonomy)
description: Extensible taxonomy of organisational context dimensions — market classification, size metrics, functional/technology/IT/AI maturity, culture, geography, market positioning, competitor bridging, and localization — enabling multi-dimensional context assignment via ORG-CONTEXT. RCSG framework entities extracted to RCSG-FW-ONT in v1.1.0.

components.ContextType: Abstract base entity for all context dimensions with category and extensibility flag
components.MarketClassification: Industry/market classification using SIC, NAICS, GICS, ICB, ISIC, NACE schemes
components.MarketSegment: Market segment with sub-sector hierarchy, addressable market sizing, and growth metrics
components.OrganisationSize: Multi-dimensional size: headcount, customers, revenue, KPI-linked metrics
components.FunctionalMaturity: Per-function maturity assessment (16 standard functions + Custom) on 1-5 scale
components.TechnologyMaturity: Cloud, digital transformation, automation maturity with IT and AI sub-types
components.ITMaturity: IT infrastructure, DevOps, security, governance sub-maturity of TechnologyMaturity
components.AIReadiness: AI data readiness, talent, deployment, ethics sub-maturity of TechnologyMaturity
components.CultureContext: Innovation orientation, risk tolerance, collaboration style, decision-making patterns
components.GeoContext: HQ location, operating regions, market reach, regulatory jurisdictions
components.MarketPositioning: Competitive position, market share, brand strength, extensible positioning criteria
components.CompetitorContext: Competitor bridging with threat level, overlap areas, strategic recommendations
components.LocalizationContext: Currency, tax, data residency, content, date/number formatting, accessibility
components.ContextExtension: User-defined custom context dimensions with schema and validation rules
components.ContextAssignment: Junction entity linking organisations to context type instances with effective dates

crossOntology.name: rcsgExtendsContextType
crossOntology.purpose: CTX <- RCSG-FW: RCSGContext extends the abstract ContextType base

versionHistory.changes: Initial release: 19 entities, 31 relationships, 14 business rules — extensible context taxonomy with market, size, maturity (functional/tech/IT/AI), culture, geo, positioning, competitor, RCSG, and localization dimensions

Extracted 4 RCSG entities (RCSGContext, RegulatoryRequirement, ComplianceFramework, GovernanceModel), 7 RCSG relationships, and 3 RCSG business rules to RCSG-FW-ONT. CTX now 15 entities, 24 internal relationships + 1 cross-ontology bridge, 11 business rules.

notes: Companion ontology to ORG-CONTEXT providing the typed context dimension taxonomy. ORG-CONTEXT references ContextTypes via cross-ontology bridges. RCSG framework entities extracted to RCSG-FW-ONT in v1.1.0. All context types extend the abstract ContextType base and support user-defined extension via ContextExtension.
